title: Workspace Component Surface Map
goal: Complete #512 by mapping current root workspace, planning, and memory surfaces to MCP-style component classes in the existing module registry contract.
non_goals: Do not implement all mapped future tools.
Do not expose source-checkout-only maintainer or dogfooding components as shipped host-repo components.
Do not add an MCP runtime or server.

intent.outcome: current shipped workspace, planning, and memory surfaces have a compact component mapping
intent.constraints: reuse the existing module registry contract; keep the root orchestrator generic and small
intent.latitude: choose whether root workspace components are top-level contract metadata rather than a selectable module
intent.escalation: escalate if satisfying #512 requires a new top-level command or runtime adapter

execution.next_step: Archive this execplan, close #512, and evaluate whether #485 can close or needs another bounded slice.
execution.proof: contract tooling, workspace CLI tests, ruff, proof, and planning doctor

invariants: root workspace is mapped as shipped orchestration components, not as a selectable module

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #485 keeps the root orchestrator thin and generic; #512 completes the current-surface mapping child of #510.

required_continuation.activation trigger: after #512 lands, evaluate whether #485 can close or whether another bounded module-boundary slice remains

delegated_judgment.hard constraints: contract-backed mapping; no MCP runtime dependency; preserve source-checkout boundary
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: exact contract field name and compact component selection
delegated_judgment.escalate when: mapping needs runtime adapter behavior or new shipped command surface

active_milestone.scope: root workspace component mapping in module registry contract plus tests

immediate_next_action: Archive this plan, close #512, refresh external evidence, and reassess #485.
invariants: No source-checkout-only maintainer tooling in shipped component map.
validation_commands: uv run ruff check src tests
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: Root workspace commands are mapped as resources/tools/prompts/schemas/roots.
Planning and memory mappings remain available from module manifests.
Mapping distinguishes shipped host-repo components from source-checkout-only components.

execution_run.what happened: Added root workspace component mapping to the existing module registry contract, exposed it through modules JSON output, and tested that root resources/tools/prompts/roots are reported separately from planning and memory module components.
execution_run.scope touched: module registry contract and schema, modules output assembly, CLI tests, contract inventory, and planning closeout state.
execution_run.changed surfaces: Existing machine contract update only; no new first-contact prose, command, runtime, or MCP server surface.
execution_run.validations run: uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; uv run ruff check src tests; uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed <paths> --format json; uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py.
execution_run.result for continuation: #512 is complete. #485 should be assessed next rather than assumed closed by this child slice alone.
execution_run.next step: Archive #512 and refresh/ingest remaining open work.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; the slice mapped current shipped surfaces without adding a runtime adapter or broad source-checkout maintainer surface.
finished_run_review.intent served: yes; root workspace, planning, and memory surfaces now have compact component mappings in one existing contract.
finished_run_review.config compliance: used compact startup/proof/doctor/defaults surfaces and did not bulk-read planning state.
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low; source-checkout-only maintainer tooling is explicitly excluded from shipped host-repo component scope.

proof_report.validation proof: contract tooling, full workspace CLI tests, lint, proof selection, CLI authority query, planning doctor, and planning surface check all passed.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: 206 workspace CLI tests passed; ruff passed; check_contract_tooling_surfaces passed; proof selected workspace_cli, cli_authority, and planning_surfaces with no blocker; planning doctor and planning surface checker were clean.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: #512 map current root, planning, and memory surfaces to MCP-style components
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: module_registry.json now declares workspace_components for shipped root resources/tools/prompts/roots while preserving planning and memory module components; schema and tests cover the new mapping.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: The package should feel smaller by making compact commands and contracts the cheapest route.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: existing contract update over new surface
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does root mapping clarify current components without making first contact heavier?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #512's mapping is complete, but #485 is a parent compression outcome and should be closed only after checking the remaining open issue set and intended outcome.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: root workspace components distinguish shipped host-repo resources/tools/prompts/roots from source-checkout-only maintainer tooling.
closure_check.reopen trigger: A shipped root workspace command/resource is found unmapped or source-checkout-only tooling appears in shipped component scope.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Root workspace components are mapped in the existing module registry alongside planning and memory module components.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: archived execplan plus GitHub closeout comment.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): none; the durable mapping belongs in the existing contract, not Memory.
execution_summary.resume from: Refresh open issues and prioritize the next lane.

improvement_signal_review.next owner: No new friction from this slice; #522 already tracks archive cleanup friction discovered in the previous closeout.

drift_log: 2026-04-28: Promoted #512 after #510/#511/#513/#514 closed and evidence refreshed.
